key_insight: SciPhyRL turns dynamic portfolio optimization into a data-driven physics-informed control problem by solving a projected Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation directly from historical trajectories.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical offline decision-making method for cost-aware, multi-period allocation problems where execution frictions, turnover, and risk must be balanced under real-world constraints.
method: Uses extended-state continuous-time reinforcement learning with cumulative costs included explicitly.
Projects the HJB equation onto observed paths and solves it with a PINN in a single offline pass.
Recasts control as a discrete target holding and models execution with quadratic price impact.
evidence_signal: On a 14-asset ETF universe with an engineered oracle signal, the learned Gibbs policy outperformed static and myopic baselines out of sample with stronger Sharpe ratio and controlled turnover/volatility.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and realism of the offline signal and market-impact assumptions, so results may be less robust when those inputs are misspecified.
paper_title: SciPhy Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization
